name: Peanut-Butter-Chocolate Shortbread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, beat margarine or butter until softened. Add powdered sugar and vanilla; mix well.
2. Gradually add flour, cocoa powder, cornstarch, and salt, stirring until smooth.
3. Fold in chopped peanuts.
4. Reserve 1.33 cups of the mixture.
5. Spread remaining mixture into the bottom and up the sides of an ungreased 10-inch tart pan with a removable bottom, or into the bottom and 1 inch up the sides of a 10-inch springform pan.
6. In another bowl, beat peanut butter and powdered sugar; stir in chocolate pieces.
7. Spread this mixture over the crust.
8. Spoon the reserved cocoa mixture over the peanut butter layer.
9. Using tines of a fork, score shortbread into 16 wedges.
10. Bake at 325°F for about 50 minutes or until slightly dry.
11. Cool slightly, then place chocolate kisses on each wedge.
12. When kisses soften (about 10 minutes), top with peanuts.
13. Score again and cool completely before removing sides of pan and cutting into wedges.
